source_of_sources_rule: WQCIU is a reference-mining and Indigenous-use framing source. For calculator values and equations, promote the underlying cited source as canonical unless the record is specifically about WQCIU contextual framing and has an exact locator.

primary_document.title: Lower Athabasca Surface Water and Sediment Quality Criteria for Protection of Indigenous Use
external_file_hints: C:\Users\jasen\OneDrive - Government of BC\My Documents\Science Projects & Groups\SABCS\2025 Sediment Standards Project\References\WQCIU Report.pdf
text_sources_checked: external PDF text extraction, not stored in repo
primary_document.limitation: The legacy markdown only contains Chapter 1 and summary tables. Equation and literature-cited locators below were checked against the external PDF.

zotero_metadata_checked.api_blocker: No Zotero API call was made in this pass; existing repo-local exports were used.

equation_leads.summary: Wildlife piscivore food-and-water benchmark equation from Sample et al. for surface water concentrations protective of mammals and birds consuming fish and water.
important_inputs: ratio of tissue concentration to water concentration

equation_leads.wqciu_locator: Chapter 3, section 3.3.5, PDF pages 153-155, Equation 3.2; local ingestion discussion at PDF pages 206-207
equation_leads.summary: Human health water criterion for traditional foods plus drinking water, modified from US EPA human-health AWQC methodology.
important_inputs: toxicity value = RfD x RSC for non-cancer effects
toxicity value = 1e-5 / CSF for cancer effects

equation_leads.wqciu_locator: Chapter 3, section 3.3.5, PDF page 156, Equation 3.3; Table 3.7 discussion at PDF pages 228-229
equation_leads.summary: Human health water criterion for medicinal plant consumption, modified from US EPA human-health AWQC methodology.
important_inputs: toxicity value = RfD x RSC for non-cancer effects
toxicity value = 1e-5 / CSF for cancer effects
medicinal plant consumption rate = 0.007 kg/day in Equation 3.3
rat root consumption rate = 0.0068 kg/day in Table 3.7 text

equation_leads.wqciu_locator: Chapter 3, section 3.4.5, PDF page 206, Equation 3.4; notes at PDF pages 205 and 227
equation_leads.summary: BaP equivalent concentration is calculated from PAH congener concentrations and Health Canada relative potency factors, then compared to BaP risk criteria.
important_inputs: Health Canada BaP relative potency factor

equation_leads.summary: US EPA equilibrium partitioning method to derive sediment quality criteria for non-carcinogenic organic contaminants from a water quality objective/guideline, Koc, organic carbon, water density, and sediment moisture.
Converts oral slope factor to a risk-specific dose using an acceptable risk level of 1e-5.
Baseline bioaccumulation factor from octanol-water partitioning coefficient and food-chain multiplier.
Freely dissolved fraction in pore water using DOC and POC. WQCIU cites NYSDEC 2014 default DOC = 0.000002 kg/L and POC = 0.

Trophic-level fish BAF derived from baseline BAF, freely dissolved fraction, and lipid content. WQCIU cites NYSDEC 2014 lipid examples of 6.46 percent for trophic level 3 and 10.31 percent for trophic level 4.
important_inputs: percent lipid in fish by trophic level

equation_leads.summary: Sediment quality criterion normalized to organic carbon, using acceptable daily intake, allocation factor, trophic-level BAF, diet fraction by trophic level, and Koc. WQCIU cites wildlife AF = 0.75 and human AF = 0.2, with no AF for carcinogenic human SQC because RsD already accounts for background exposure.

parameter_value_leads.applicability: Median open-water Old Fort values from 2011-2019; not a generic BC default.

parameter_value_leads.wqciu_locator: Chapter 3, section 3.4.5, PDF pages 206-207; Equation 3.2 at PDF page 155
parameter_value_leads.summary: Adopted fish ingestion rate from the highest calculated community rate, using the upper range from traditional food survey results.

parameter_value_leads.wqciu_locator: Chapter 3, Equation 3.3 at PDF page 156; Table 3.7 text at PDF page 228; discussion at PDF page 207
parameter_value_leads.summary: Rat root consumption rate adopted as the medicinal plant ingestion rate. Equation 3.3 displays 0.007 kg/day, which appears to be rounded.

parameter_value_leads.wqciu_locator: Chapter 1 summary Table 1.2 and Table 1.3; detailed water criteria in Tables 3.5-3.7; detailed sediment criteria in Table 4.1

parameter_value_leads.review_notes: Reference values only. Each row needs exact canonical source locators before promotion into parameter_values.json or equation-backed defaults.

canonical_source_leads.title_from_wqciu: Methodology for Deriving Ambient Water Quality Criteria for the Protection of Human Health
canonical_source_leads.role: Canonical method for human-health AWQC equations modified by WQCIU.

canonical_source_leads.role: Canonical source for HH DW+Org criteria that WQCIU modified for community fish consumption.

canonical_source_leads.role: Canonical source for BAFs, BCFs, food-chain multipliers, and lipid fractions for organic substances.

next_actions: Use Zotero UI/API metadata lookup to add or deduplicate unmatched high-priority targets.
For each calculator value, promote only the underlying canonical source with exact table/page/equation locator.
Do not promote WQCIU table values as generic BC calculator defaults without owner review of applicability.
Use WQCIU's fish ingestion and medicinal plant rates only as WQCIU/local Indigenous-use scenario inputs unless owner approves broader defaults.
